sections.subtitle: Current Form
sections.text: FCV Dender are struggling, sitting at the bottom of the league with no wins in their last 5 matches, managing only 2 draws and conceding an average of 2 goals per game. SV Zulte Waregem, in contrast, show mixed form with 2 wins, 2 draws, and 1 loss in their last 5, scoring consistently but also conceding in most games. Their recent 4-1 victory over KAA Gent highlights their attacking potential.

sections.subtitle: Head-to-Head Record
sections.text: In the last two encounters, SV Zulte Waregem have dominated FCV Dender, winning both matches (2-1 and 4-1). Dender have struggled to contain Waregem's attack, conceding multiple goals in each game, which aligns with their defensive frailty shown in recent matches.

sections.subtitle: Season Statistics
sections.text: Dender's season stats reveal a poor offensive output with only 6 goals scored (lowest in the league) and 21 conceded, alongside a low corner average (3.08 per game). Zulte Waregem, positioned 8th, have a balanced record with 19 goals scored and conceded, showing capability in attack but vulnerability in defense, which could lead to an open game.

sections.subtitle: Key Insights
sections.text: Dender's inability to keep clean sheets (none in last 5 matches) and Waregem's tendency to score late in games (26% of goals in the 76-90 minute range) could be decisive. With Sofascore predicting a 51% chance of an away win, Waregem's superior league standing and head-to-head record make them favorites.

halftimeSections.subtitle: First half overview
halftimeSections.text: The first half saw FCV Dender take a narrow lead over SV Zulte Waregem with a score of 1-0. The home side capitalized on an early opportunity, with Bruny Nsimba finding the back of the net in the 13th minute. Dender showed efficiency in front of goal, converting one of their notable chances. Meanwhile, Zulte Waregem struggled to turn their possession into clear-cut opportunities, missing a big chance and failing to test the goalkeeper frequently. Both teams displayed discipline, with just one yellow card apiece, and no red cards to disrupt the flow of the game.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Team performance breakdown
halftimeSections.text: FCV Dender demonstrated a strong presence in the attacking third, with multiple entries into the penalty area and a decent rate of successful duels won. Their ability to win possession in dangerous areas also helped them maintain pressure at key moments. On the other hand, SV Zulte Waregem held a slight edge in ball possession and had more entries into the final third, but their attacking output lacked the necessary sharpness. Defensively, both sides held firm, with Dender showing better duel-winning stats, while Zulte Waregem dominated aerial challenges.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Key players and contributions
halftimeSections.text: Bruny Nsimba was the standout performer for FCV Dender, not only scoring the crucial goal but also posing a constant threat in attack. Noah Mbamba also played a pivotal role with an assist, showcasing creativity in the final third. For SV Zulte Waregem, Anosike Ementa was involved in creating a big chance, though the team couldn’t capitalize on it. Defensively, players like Malcolm Viltard for Dender and Yannick Cappelle for Zulte Waregem made important contributions with clearances and interceptions to keep their respective sides in the game.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Attack momentum analysis
halftimeSections.text: The momentum in the first half saw significant shifts between the two teams. Initially, SV Zulte Waregem started with strong attacking intent, dominating the early minutes with consistent forward pressure. However, FCV Dender took control around the time of their goal, embarking on several dangerous attacking spells that pinned Zulte Waregem back. Towards the latter part of the half, momentum swung back and forth, with Dender enjoying prolonged periods of dominance in the middle stages, while Zulte Waregem pushed hard in the closing minutes to level the score.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Second half expectations
halftimeSections.text: Looking ahead to the second half, FCV Dender will likely aim to protect their lead while exploiting counter-attacking opportunities, given their effectiveness in the final third. SV Zulte Waregem, trailing by a goal, are expected to come out with renewed intensity, pushing for an equalizer and potentially taking more risks in attack. If Dender can maintain their defensive structure, they might capitalize on Zulte Waregem’s urgency to create spaces at the back. Expect a competitive half, with both sides having chances to influence the outcome.
